title: Logging and Debug Infrastructure
description: Toggling log categories, prefixing log output, and aggregating logs for debugging

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: Calls DebugService.logs(category) to enable a log category at runtime

actors.name: DebugService
actors.description: GUI service (extends Service) registered as 'debug'. Manages enabled_logs[], broadcasts toggle to iframes, registers params with ExecService

actors.name: ExecService
actors.description: Receives param providers from DebugService; appends enabled_logs as query param

actors.name: IframeApps
actors.description: Child iframes that receive postMessage { $: 'puterjs-debug', cmd: 'log.on', category } to sync log state

use_cases.name: Enable Log Category
use_cases.description: Developer calls DebugService.logs(category); adds to enabled_logs[], calls puter.logger.on(category), broadcasts postMessage to all iframes

use_cases.name: Toggle Category On (Logger)
use_cases.description: CategorizedToggleLogger.on(category) sets categories[category]=true, enabling pass-through for that category

use_cases.name: Toggle Category Off (Logger)
use_cases.description: CategorizedToggleLogger.off(category) deletes categories[category], silencing that category

use_cases.name: Filter Log by Category
use_cases.description: On log(level, msg, fields, values): checks fields.category against enabled categories map; delegates to next logger only if enabled

use_cases.name: Prefix Log Message
use_cases.description: Prepends this.prefix to message before delegating: delegate.log(level, prefix+message, fields, values)

use_cases.name: Output Log to Console
use_cases.description: Formats message with ANSI color codes per level (info=green, warn=yellow, error=red, debug=blue), renders fields as key=value pairs, calls console.log/error

use_cases.name: Aggregate Logs in Buffer
use_cases.description: Stores {level, message, fields, values} objects into internal buffer array — used for testing/later processing

use_cases.name: Expose Enabled Logs as Query Param
use_cases.description: During _init, registers a param provider with ExecService that returns { enabled_logs: 'cat1;cat2' } when categories are enabled

use_cases.name: Propagate Log Toggle to Iframes
use_cases.description: After enabling a category, iterates all <iframe> elements and posts { $: 'puterjs-debug', cmd: 'log.on', category } to each contentWindow

use_cases.name: Chain Logger with Fields
use_cases.description: LoggerFacade.fields(fields) creates a FieldsLogger wrapping current impl, returns new LoggerFacade — supports fluent method chaining
use_cases.method: LoggerFacade.fields(fields) → new FieldsLogger({ fields, delegate: impl })

key_classes.name: LoggerFacade
key_classes.notes: puter.logger is a LoggerFacade; on/off delegate to cat (CategorizedToggleLogger)

key_classes.name: FieldsLogger
key_classes.notes: Merges default fields with per-call fields via Object.assign

key_relationships: LoggerFacade delegates_to TLogger impl (for log output)
All logger classes implement TLogger interface
Loggers compose via decorator/chain pattern (delegate property)
important_notes: All logger classes use AdvancedBase with static PROPERTIES (for property injection) and static IMPLEMENTS (for trait/interface binding via TLogger). Logger pipeline is composable: e.g. PrefixLogger → CategorizedToggleLogger → ConsoleLogger. DebugService.logs() syncs state to both the main-window puter.logger and all iframe child windows via postMessage. enabled_logs array is serialized as semicolon-separated string for query params.
